user_prompt: to `BtHostList`, add a method to insert a new entry, and have BluetoothManager call it when pairing is successful
agent_reply: bool insert(const char* name, const esp_bd_addr_t bdaddr);
- Appends a new host if the MAC is not already in the list.
- If the MAC already exists, it refreshes `bonded`.
- It preserves an existing non-empty name, so a JSON-edited friendly name stays authoritative.
- If the existing name is empty and pairing provides one, it fills it in.
- Allocation failure is detectable via `false` and `lastResult()`.
`BluetoothManager` now owns a singleton host list:
Build succeeded. Note: `BtHostList.h/.cpp` are still untracked new files, so remember to add them when committing.

user_prompt: `BtHostList` needs a save method, save to the same JSON file. Assume the microSD card can never be ejected, and only block this call if the destructor has been called or if the list is empty
agent_reply: - Saves to the same remembered path used by `loadFromMicroSd()`.
- Defaults to `/bluetooth.json` if no load has happened yet.
- Writes the current linked list as pretty JSON with a `hosts` array.
- Refuses to save only when the list is empty or the destructor has marked the object destroyed.

I did not auto-save from the Bluetooth pairing callback, because if the list had not been loaded first, that could overwrite an existing `bluetooth.json` with only the newly paired device. The pairing callback still inserts into `BtManager::hostList()`.
Build succeeded. `BtHostList.h/.cpp` are still untracked new files.

user_prompt: I want to run `void test_sdcard()` to test performance of the recorder with encryption enabled
make sure everything is wired so that it works with encryption actually built and active
agent_reply: Wired `test_sdcard()` so it should actually test encrypted recorder performance now.
- `setup()` now calls `all_init()` before bring-up tests, so NVS/M5/gui basics exist before `Aegis` and SD setup.
- installs a deterministic test master key only if no key already exists,
- forces `AudioFileRecorder::setPurePcmMode(false)` so it does not bypass encryption.
- `AudioFileRecorder` now includes `Aegis` by relative path so PlatformIO library discovery works.
- `BtHostList.h` and `WifiManager.h` now include `conf.h`, so their security-mode structs match their `.cpp` files.
Build passed. I restored the generated `src/version.c` bump afterward.
